Finance Ministry Shifts 213 Staff to Tax Directorate to Boost Collection Capacity
body: Indonesia will reassign 213 staff to the tax office to meet higher revenue targets without increasing hiring or fiscal burden.

title: IDX Extends Short-Selling Ban
body: Indonesia extends short-selling ban as global tensions and oil-driven volatility hit markets, with JCI sliding to 7,097 on foreign outflows.

title: JCI Stays in the Red Zone on Weekend Close, Slips to 7,097
body: JCI fell 0.94% on Friday as geopolitical tensions, volatile oil prices, and heavy foreign outflows pressured sentiment.

title: Bahlil: No Plan to Limit Fuel Subsidies Despite Malaysia Move
body: Indonesia will keep fuel subsidies unchanged despite Malaysia’s cuts.

title: Finance Ministry Explains Purbaya’s Rp 50 M Tax Shortfall
body: Finance Ministry says Purbaya’s Rp 50M tax shortfall stems from multiple income sources.

title: Alfamart Books $7.5 Billion Revenue in 2025
body: Alfamart posted more than 7% revenue growth in 2025, but rising costs continued to pressure margins, even as net profit rose 8.34%.

title: Iran War: Indonesian Palm Oil Price to Rise, But Importers Might Hold Back
body: If this war doesn’t last too long, the price hike on Indonesian palm oil might not be too significant, a think-tank says.

title: Pertamina Tankers Still Stuck in Persian Gulf as Malaysia Gets Hormuz Pass
body: Iran has blocked the Strait of Hormuz in retaliation for the US-Israeli strikes on Tehran in late February.

title: Gold Slump Deepens on Friday: Antam Down Rp 40,000, Buyback Plunges
body: Antam gold fell Rp 40,000 on Friday, tracking a global decline as rising US yields and a stronger dollar pressured bullion prices.

title: JCI Extends Friday Losses as Oil Surge and Foreign Sell-Off Weigh
body: JCI extended losses as oil prices surged and geopolitical tensions fueled foreign outflows.

title: Can Indonesia Keep Its Deficit Below 3% as Oil Prices Climb?
body: Early-year data suggest a cautious approach: state spending reached Rp 493.8 trillion ($29.2 billion) as of Feb. 28.

title: Bahlil Warns Against Panic Buying as Energy Pressures Mount
body: Energy minister urges prudent fuel and LPG use, warning against panic buying as global oil supply disruptions pressure energy markets.

title: No More Dollar: China-ASEAN Cross-Border Yuan Use Nears $1.3 Trillion
body: China-ASEAN cross-border yuan settlement jumped over 50% year-on-year in 2024.

title: Finance Minister Urges Critics to 'Go Back to School' Over Economy Concerns
body: Finance minister rebukes economists for alarmist views, urging data-based analysis amid concerns over rising oil prices.

title: Indonesia Opens Door to Higher Coal, Nickel Output
body: Indonesia may relax coal and nickel output caps if global prices stay strong, as smelter demand outpaces current quotas.

title: Indonesia’s Coretax Proves Too Complex, Even for Its Finance Minister
body: Indonesia extends tax deadline to April as minister admits Coretax issues, citing glitches, poor design, and user complaints.

title: Rupiah Strengthens Post-Eid as Iran-US Signals Temper Market Nerves
body: Rupiah strengthens to Rp 16,904 per dollar as Iran-US signals offset oil volatility and lingering Middle East tensions.

title: Oil Rally Spurs Market Sell-Off as Indonesia Stocks Drop 1.89%
body: JCI drops 1.89% as oil nears $100 and Iran tensions escalate, offsetting Ramadan-driven optimism and domestic resilience.

title: Higher-Grade Fuel Demand Surges During Eid Travel Rush
body: Pertamax demand rises 11.8% ahead of Eid as travel surges, with higher-grade fuel gaining traction among motorists for long-distance trips.

title: Indonesia Eyes April 1 Launch for Coal Levy as Budget Pressures Mount
body: Indonesia targets an April coal export levy to capture windfall profits and ease fiscal pressure from rising energy subsidy costs.
